Hazards & Risks:
Manual Handling
Ergonomic from repeated:
- Kneeing / bending
- Moving robot elements
Current Risk Controls:
Comply with the Booster P&ERA: AIIL-Booster-K1
Comply with RMIT manual handling policies.
If tilting the case from the wheels to a lying position (or vice versa) bend with the knees and with a straight back.
Additional Risk Control Measures:
Prefer wheeling the case over carrying.
Only carry the case via the fixed handles. The pop-up handle may only be used for moving the case on the wheels.
Do not carry the case above shoulder height.
Responsibility:
Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor)
All operators and users

Hazards & Risks:
Manual Handling
Ergonomic from repeated:
- Kneeing / bending
- Moving robot elements
Current Risk Controls:
Move the K1 following the RA: AIIL-Booster-K1-MH
Comply with the Booster P&ERA: AIIL-Booster-K1
Additional Risk Control Measures:
When unpacking, either (1) place the robot face-down on a flat ground surface or (2) use the Booster stool (located in the transport case) to sit the robot.
When packing, place the robot into the case face-first.
Responsibility:
Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor)
All operators and users

Hazards & Risks:
Electrical:
- Charging
- Electrical Shock
- Battery Failure
Current Risk Controls:
Charge the battery unit according to the Booster Manual.
Comply with the Booster P&ERA: AIIL-Booster-K1
Monitor the battery charging status and battery unit.

Activity or Task: Insertion/removal of the battery into the robot
Hazards & Risks:
Manual Handling
Ergonomic from repeated:
- Kneeing / bending
- Moving robot elements
Other:
- Injury
Electrical:
- Electrical Shock
Current Risk Controls:
Insert the battery unit according to the Booster Manual.
Comply with the Booster P&ERA: AIIL-Booster-K1
Ensure the robot is either (1) placed face-down on a flat surface with hands and legs in a natural posture before inserting the battery unit; or (2) naturally seated on its chair.
The battery can be inserted only one direction - the side-labels facing upwards.
To remove the battery, the sliding locks must be squeezed. Do not force the sliding locks.
Responsibility:
Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor)
All operators and users

Activity or Task: Activation of soft Software Emergency Stop
Hazards & Risks:
Circumstances, including elsewhere listed in this RA, requiring activation of the soft Software emergency stop functionality.
Motion:
- Injury from collision with the robot
- Injury from an emergency requirement to handle the robot while in motion.
Object:
- Collision with other objects
Other:
- Injury
- Unexpected robot movement
Current Risk Controls:
Activate soft software E-STOP by placing the K1 in PREP mode.
Comply with the Booster P&ERA: AIIL-Booster-K1.
Activate PREP via the K1 PREP button (on the K1's back) or joystick shortcut according to the Booster Manual.
Monitor the K1 to ensure it comes to a stop. Monitor the K1 to in case it falls if activating PREP mode causes instability.
Responsibility:
Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor)
All operators and users
